name: Ginger
summary: A pungent root — brewed as tea or eaten fresh — with one of the strongest evidence bases in all of food science for one thing: settling nausea.

benefits.mechanism: Ginger's gingerols and shogaols act on serotonin (5-HT3) receptors in the gut and on gastric motility, which is why it eases nausea. Meta-analyses of randomized trials find it effective for pregnancy-related nausea, and other reviews support it for post-operative and motion sickness.

citations.title: A systematic review and meta-analysis of the effect and safety of ginger in the treatment of pregnancy-associated nausea and vomiting (Viljoen et al., Nutr J 2014)

surprisingFacts.text: Cooking transforms ginger's sharp gingerol into zingerone — milder and sweeter — which is why baked ginger tastes gentler than raw.
